techniques.evidence_quote: the web server was vulnerable to CVE-2021-40438. This Server Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability allowed attackers to send HTTP requests to hosts behind the firewall.

techniques.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.evidence_quote: Coupled with the usage of outdated Instance Metadata Service Version 1 (IMDSv1), the SSRF vulnerability allowed the threat actor to exfiltrate temporary credentials associated with the VM instance.
A hard-coded GitHub credential in one VM instance's user data allowed the threat actor to access the source code repositories
exfiltrate temporary credentials associated with the VM instance
The obtained cloud credentials enabled the threat actor to use control plane APIs to carry out the rest of the attack.
The threat actor created a backdoor IAM role that allowed access from an attacker-controlled account.
created a backdoor IAM role that allowed access from an attacker-controlled account
A cloud access key hard-coded in a CI/CD automation script granted the attacker administrator permission.
The threat actor enumerated and viewed all the VM instances' user data.

techniques.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
techniques.evidence_quote: The obtained cloud credentials enabled the threat actor to use control plane APIs

techniques.technique_name: Data from Information Repositories: Code Repositories
techniques.evidence_quote: The threat actor cloned all the repositories in the company's GitHub organization.

techniques.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
techniques.evidence_quote: The threat actor used infrastructure as code (IaC) service CloudFormation to deploy resources needed for performing cryptojacking (e.g., VPC, subnet, firewalls, and VM instances).
The threat actor deployed hundreds of GPU-based VM instances, costing the company $12,000 daily.
The threat actor deployed botnet malware on the compromised hosts and launched DDoS attacks from the company's network.

attack_chain.description: Threat actor discovered an internal web server that was exposed to the internet due to a misconfigured security group, and exploited CVE-2021-40438 (SSRF) on it.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: An internal web server was accidentally made public due to a misconfigured security group setting... the web server was vulnerable to CVE-2021-40438. This Server Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: Used the SSRF vulnerability combined with IMDSv1 to exfiltrate temporary IAM credentials from the EC2 instance metadata service.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Coupled with the usage of outdated Instance Metadata Service Version 1 (IMDSv1), the SSRF vulnerability allowed the threat actor to exfiltrate temporary credentials associated with the VM instance.

attack_chain.description: Used the stolen temporary IAM credentials to authenticate to AWS control plane APIs.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The obtained cloud credentials enabled the threat actor to use control plane APIs to carry out the rest of the attack.

attack_chain.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
attack_chain.description: Deployed attacker-controlled cloud resources (VPC, subnets, firewalls, VM instances) via CloudFormation to support cryptojacking operations.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor used infrastructure as code (IaC) service CloudFormation to deploy resources needed for performing cryptojacking (e.g., VPC, subnet, firewalls, and VM instances).

attack_chain.description: Created a backdoor IAM role allowing access from an attacker-controlled AWS account to maintain persistence.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor created a backdoor IAM role that allowed access from an attacker-controlled account.

attack_chain.description: Enumerated all VM instances and viewed their user data to search for sensitive information and credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor enumerated and viewed all the VM instances' user data.

attack_chain.description: Found a hard-coded GitHub credential in an EC2 instance's user data, enabling access to the company's source code repositories.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: A hard-coded GitHub credential in one VM instance's user data allowed the threat actor to access the source code repositories for the entire company.

attack_chain.technique_name: Data from Information Repositories: Code Repositories
attack_chain.description: Cloned all repositories in the company's GitHub organization and scanned them for additional credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor cloned all the repositories and scanned for more credentials.

attack_chain.description: Discovered and used a hard-coded AWS access key from a CI/CD automation script granting administrator permissions, escalating privileges in the AWS account.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: A cloud access key hard-coded in a CI/CD automation script granted the attacker administrator permission.

attack_chain.description: Deployed hundreds of GPU-based EC2 instances for cryptomining, costing the victim $12,000/day.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor deployed hundreds of GPU-based VM instances, costing the company $12,000 daily.

attack_chain.description: Deployed botnet malware on compromised hosts and launched outbound DDoS attacks from the victim's cloud network.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor deployed botnet malware on the compromised hosts and launched DDoS attacks from the company's network.
